title: Coveney (double Lancaster crash) Lancaster Memorial
narrative: Lancaster ED503 took off from RAF Waddington on 29 January 1943 for an engine and airframe test flight. Over the flat fenland near Boston, the aircraft suddenly lost altitude for reasons that were never determined and plunged into the waterlogged ground at Sibsey Northlands. The impact created a deep crater in the quicksand and subsoil. The fire service spent two days attempting to pump out the water and recover the crew, but it proved impossible. The fen simply swallowed the aircraft and five of its six crew members. Only Sgt Thomas Wishart, who was thrown clear of the wreckage on impact, could be recovered. He was buried in his home town of Dalkeith, Scotland. His five crewmates remain where they fell, entombed in the Lincolnshire fenland. Because their bodies were never recovered, the five are commemorated on the Runnymede Memorial. After the war, the families of the crew paid for a stone cross memorial to be erected at the crash site. It was consecrated in 1947 and bears all six names. The memorial stands alone in open fenland -- a solitary cross in a vast flat landscape, marking where five young men lie forever.
memorial_details: Stone cross memorial erected 1947, funded by the crew's families. Located in open fenland at Bishops Farm, Sibsey Northlands. The Sibsey Lancaster Memorial Trust (associated with Coningsby Town Council) maintains the site and organises annual commemorations, regularly attended by 200+ people, with wreaths from the Royal British Legion, RAFA, and RAF Coningsby. The Mareham Le Fen Silver Band provides music. Condition: good, actively maintained.

external_narratives.summary: Community archive record of the double Lancaster loss in Coveney parish on 19 April 1944. Lancasters LL667 and LL867 of 115 Squadron were shot down by Luftwaffe Me 410 intruders as they returned to RAF Witchford from a raid on Rouen, crashing within a mile of their home base.
key_details: Both shot down by Me 410 intruders
LL667 crashed at Common Farm, Witchford at 02:10

external_narratives.summary: Memorial plaque record for the two 115 Squadron Lancaster crews at Coveney. The memorial in the village church commemorates all 14 aircrew killed in the two crashes on the night of 19 April 1944.
key_details: Memorial plaque in Coveney village church

external_narratives.summary: Accident record for Lancaster Mk II LL667 of 115 Squadron shot down on 19 April 1944. Documents the loss as combat-related, intercepted by night intruder aircraft.
Squadron records for Lancaster LL667 KO-R of 115 Squadron. Provides crew listing and operational history of the aircraft prior to its loss on the night of 19 April 1944.
